input.new_string:
/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property

The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.

Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
unchanged. -/

/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
  F : T.Modules
  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
  epi : Epi q
  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d

/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
coincide). -/
def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q

lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩

lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩

lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g, by rw [Iso.trans_hom, ← Category.assoc, hf, hg]⟩

/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
  r := RankQuotient.Rel
  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩

/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
  epi := by
    haveI := x.epi
    haveI : Epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) := inferInstance
    exact epi_comp _ _
  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree

/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/
lemma rqPullback_rel {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
    (rqPullback ψ x).Rel (rqPullback ψ y) := by
  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).mapIso f, ?_⟩
  show ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) ≫
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map f.hom
    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map y.q
  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map_comp, hf]

/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.

The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
`map_comp`) are reduced — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — to the free-sheaf coherences
`pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 T) = (pullbackId T).app (free _)` and the analogue for a composite,
which in turn reduce to the unit-level identity `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) =
(pullbackId).app unit` (and its composite analogue). These remaining coherences between
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` are
`whnf`-heavy and left as the sole open obstacle. -/
noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
  map {X Y} g := Quotient.map (rqPullback g.unop) (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h)
  map_id X := by
    funext z
    induction z using Quotient.ind with
    | _ x =>
      refine Quotient.sound ?_
      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` from `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation
      -- reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`.
      refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
      show ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
          ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F).hom = x.q
      rw [Category.assoc, ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom).naturality x.q]
      -- remaining: `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) .inv ≫ (pullbackId.app free).hom = 𝟙`
      sorry
  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
    funext z
    induction z using Quotient.ind with
    | _ x =>
      refine Quotient.sound ?_
      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` from
      -- `pullbackComp`; the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite
      -- free coherence relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to the composite.
      refine ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
      sorry

def yoneda : C ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type v₁ where
  obj X :=
    { obj Y := (unop Y) ⟶ X
      map f := TypeCat.ofHom fun g ↦ f.unop ≫ g }
  map f :=
    { app _ := TypeCat.ofHom fun g ↦ g ≫ f }

/-- Unification hint for `(yoneda.obj X).obj (op Y) = Y ⟶ X`. -/
unif_hint yoneda_obj_obj_eq_hom (X X' Y Y' : C) where
  X ≟ X'
  Y ≟ Y' ⊢
  (yoneda.obj X).obj (op Y) ≟ Y' ⟶ X'
The `Type` category now wraps morphisms via `TypeCat.ofHom`. Let me use that idiom.

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/GrassmannianQuot.lean

Iter-053. Assigned: drop ≥1 of the 5 scaffold sorries
(`glue`/`universalQuotient`/`tautologicalQuotient`/`functor`/`represents`); `functor` is the
glue-independent, "likely cheaper" target.

## Headline: the keystone gap blocking `functor` is now CLOSED

`functor` (the contravariant functor of points) acts on morphisms by pullback, and that needs
`f^*(O^r) ≅ O^r` for an **arbitrary** scheme morphism `f`. Mathlib's
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` supplies this only under `[F.Final]` where `F = Opens.map
f.base`; previous chapters discharged `Final` *only for open immersions / isos*
(`opensMap_final_of_schemeIso` in QuotScheme). For a general `f` it was an open obstacle — I
confirmed `(Opens.map φ.base).Final`, `(pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I` and
`(pullback φ).obj (unit) ≅ unit` are all NOT instances and `pullbackObjFreeIso` fails to
synthesize `Final` for a general morphism.

**`opensMap_final` closes it for every scheme morphism.** Over any open `V` of the target the
structured-arrow category `{U : V ≤ φ⁻¹U}` has the terminal object `⊤`, hence is connected, so
`Opens.map φ.base` is `Final`. Fully proven (`zigzag_isConnected` + a terminal `StructuredArrow`).
This single lemma unlocks `f^* free ≅ free` for the whole category of schemes.

## New, fully-proven (0-sorry) declarations

In `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules`:
- `opensMap_final {T' T} (φ : T' ⟶ T) : (Opens.map φ.base).Final` — the keystone above.
- `pullbackFreeIso (φ) (I) : (pullback φ).obj (free I) ≅ free I` — general `f^* free ≅ free`.
- `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank (φ) (h : IsLocallyFreeOfRank M d) : IsLocallyFreeOfRank
  ((pullback φ).obj M) d` — pullback preserves rank-`d` local freeness (cover `{φ⁻¹U_i}`,
  `pullbackComp` + `morphismRestrict_ι` factorisation + `pullbackFreeIso`).

In `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian` (before `functor`):
- `structure RankQuotient r d T` (F, q, epi, locFree) — one rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`.
- `RankQuotient.Rel` + `rel_refl`/`rel_symm`/`rel_trans` + `instance rqSetoid` — the
  equivalence (iso commuting with the quotient maps).
- `rqPullback (ψ) (x) : RankQuotient r d T'` — the pullback action (epi preserved via the
  left-adjoint; locFree via `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`).
- `rqPullback_rel` — pullback respects `Rel`, so it descends to the quotient.

## `functor` — fully ASSEMBLED (obj + map proven), laws reduced to one named obstacle

`functor d r : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1` is now a complete `Functor` literal:
- `obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)` ✓
- `map g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback g.unop) rqPullback_rel)` ✓
- `map_id` / `map_comp`: reduced (via `ext`/`Quotient.ind`/`change`/`Quotient.sound`) to the
  equivalence on representatives, with the **canonical witnessing isos supplied**
  (`pullbackId.app x.F` and `(pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F |>.symm`). The residual
  q-coherence is `sorry`.

**Signature correction (forced, not a weakening):** the value is `Type 1`, not the scaffold's
`Type` — a `RankQuotient` bundles `F : T.Modules`, a *large* object, so its quotient is in
`Type 1`. Verified directly (`RankQuotient ... : Type 1`). `represents` still typechecks against
the `Type 1` functor (build is green). Unfolding `functor` exposes the genuine
rank-`d`-quotients quotient — the litmus test passes.

## The single remaining obstacle (both laws)

Both q-coherences reduce, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom` / `pullbackComp.hom` (term-mode), and
then a coproduct-`ext` over `free = ∐ unit` using the `@[simp]`
`pullback_map_ιFree_comp_pullbackObjFreeIso_hom`, to the **unit-level identity**
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = ((pullbackId).app unit).hom` (and a composite
analogue through `pullbackComp`). I reduced this cleanly to
`unitToPushforwardObjUnit (𝟙) = adj.homEquiv ((pullbackId.app unit).hom)` via
`rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit, Equiv.symm_apply_eq, Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]` (no whnf blow-up),
but closing it requires navigating `pullbackId = leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId)` and
`conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom` — a genuine multi-lemma coherence development between
`SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId`/`pullbackComp`. `simp [pullbackId]`
whnf-times-out (consistent with memory `quot-gap1-closed-opaque-immersion`).

## Dead ends / gotchas (for the next prover)

- The `T.Modules` def-diamond breaks **all** positional category `rw`/`simp`/instance search in
  the FILE context (it is laxer in `lean_run_code`!): `← Category.assoc`, `rw [hf]` inside a
  `map (...)`, and even `Epi`-instance synthesis (`x.epi` won't register via `haveI`). Fixes used:
  term-mode `congrArg`/`.trans`/`Category.assoc` for the setoid lemmas, and a fully-`@`-explicit
  `epi_comp`/`Functor.map_epi (… inferInstance … x.epi)` for `rqPullback.epi`.
- `Type`-category morphisms now wrap via `TypeCat.ofHom` — a bare `Quotient.map` (a `→`) is
  rejected as the functor `map`; the law goals need `ext`/`Quotient.ind` (raw `funext` fails on
  `TypeCat.ofHom _ = 𝟙`).
- `Scheme.Hom.iSup_preimage_eq_top` and `morphismRestrict_ι` are the exact factorisation lemmas
  for `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`.

## Concrete next step to DROP `functor`

Prove the helper `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙 T).toRingCatSheafHom = ((pullbackId T).app unit).hom`
(and the `pullbackComp` analogue `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (f ≫ g) = … ≫ pullbackObjUnitToUnit …`),
then `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)` follows by coproduct-`ext`, and both
functor laws close in term-mode (naturality already verified to apply). This is a self-contained
`SheafOfModules`-coherence task using `Adjunction.homEquiv_unit` + `conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom`
+ `leftAdjointIdIso`.

## Blueprint flags (for plan/review agents — I do not edit chapters)

- `def:grassmannian_functor` (`functor`) is now formalised but still uses `sorry` (laws) — NOT
  `\leanok`-ready.
- The new helpers want `\lean{}` hints + statement blocks:
  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final`, `…pullbackFreeIso`,
  `…pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`, `…Grassmannian.RankQuotient`, `…rqPullback`. `opensMap_final`
  is a genuinely reusable, broadly-applicable lemma (general "pullback of free is free") that
  likely helps the GF and QUOT lanes too.

## Summary

- **Sorry count (declaration-level): 5 → 5.** I did NOT fully close a declaration. The five
  scaffold declarations (`glue`/`universalQuotient`/`tautologicalQuotient`/`functor`/`represents`)
  still each use `sorry`. (`functor` now contains 2 *internal* law-sorries vs 1 before, so the raw
  `sorry`-token count is 5 → 6, but the `functor` *declaration* went from a bare `:= sorry` to a
  fully-assembled `Functor` with only its two coherence laws open.)
- **Closed (0-sorry, new):** `opensMap_final`, `pullbackFreeIso`, `pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`,
  `RankQuotient` (+ `Rel`/refl/symm/trans/`rqSetoid`), `rqPullback`, `rqPullback_rel`.
- **Still open:** `glue` (module descent — untouched, separate hard lane), `universalQuotient`/
  `tautologicalQuotient`/`represents` (ride on `glue`), and `functor`'s two law q-coherences
  (reduced to the named unit coherence).
- **Adjacent sorries attempted beyond assigned:** `functor` was the assigned target; I did not
  touch `glue`/`universalQuotient`/`tautologicalQuotient`/`represents` (all gated on `glue`,
  out of reach this session).

## Why I stopped

**Partial progress** — measurable Lean code, not prose. I closed the keystone Mathlib gap that
blocked the entire `functor` lane (`opensMap_final` ⟹ general `f^* free ≅ free`) with three
fully-proven, reusable lemmas, then built every gap-free part of `functor` (structure, setoid,
pullback action + well-definedness, the `obj` and `map` of the functor itself) as compiling
0-sorry code, and reduced both functoriality laws to a single, precisely-named coherence
(`pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`) with the canonical witnessing isos already
in place.

I did **not** meet the literal "drop ≥1 declaration sorry": closing `functor` requires the unit
coherence between `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId`/`pullbackComp`,
which is a genuine multi-lemma coherence development (the `leftAdjointIdIso`/`conjugateEquiv`
chain; `simp [pullbackId]` whnf-times-out). I attempted it via the adjunction route
(`homEquiv_unit` + `Equiv.symm_apply_eq`, which avoids the blow-up) and reached the reduced goal,
but could not close it blindly in the remaining budget. The other four declarations all depend on
`glue` (module descent over a scheme glue datum), which is a separate, larger construction I did
not start. No LLM API key in env, so the informal agent was unavailable (per PROGRESS standing
notes); I used Mathlib search + the blueprint instead.

## Headline: closed the keystone gap that blocked the entire `functor` lane
